title: centaurea margaritacea subsp. breviceps
intro: Centaurea margaritacea subsp. breviceps, also known as a plant species within the family Asteraceae, is a type of wildflower that is native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. Its health benefits include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. Centaurea margaritacea subsp. breviceps can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies and supplements to harness its medicinal properties.
